verses.t: my children, observe the mitsvot of Yahuah, and honour Lewi and Yahuḏah; for from them shall arise unto you [the Lamb of Aluahim, who taketh away the sin of the world] one who saveth [all the Gentiles and] Yasharal. 12 For His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, which shall not pass away; but my kingdom among you shall come to an end as a watcher's hammock, which after the summer disappeareth. 20 1For I know that after my death the Mitsrites will afflict you, but Aluahim will avenge you, and will 2 bring you into that which He promised to your fathers. But ye shall carry up my bones with you; for when my bones are being taken up thither, Yahuah shall be with you in light, and Beliar shall be in darkness with the Mitsrites. 3 And carry ye up Asenath your mother to the Hippodrome, and near Rachel your mother bury her. 4, 5 And when he had said these things he stretched out his feet, and died at a good old age. And all Yasharal mourned for him, and all Mitsrayim, with a great mourning. 6 And when the children of Yasharal went out of Mitsrayim, they took with them the bones of Yoseph, and they buried him in Hebron with his fathers, and the years of his life were one hundred and ten years. 1 1 The copy of the words of Binyamin, which he commanded his sons to observe, after he had lived 2 a hundred and twenty-five years. And he kissed them, and said: As Yitsḥaq was born to Aḇraham 3 in his old age, so also was I to Ya'aqoḇ. And since Rachel my mother died in giving me birth, I had 4 no milk; therefore I was suckled by Bilhah her handmaid. For Rachel remained barren for twelve years after she had borne Yoseph; and she prayed Yahuah with fasting twelve days, and she 5 conceived and bare me. For my father loved Rachel dearly, and prayed that he might see two 6 sons born from her. Therefore was I called Binyamin, that is, a son of days. 2 1 And when I went into Mitsrayim, to Yoseph, and my brother recognized me, he said unto me: 2 What did they tell my father when they sold me ? And I said unto him, They dabbled thy coat with blood and sent it, and said: Know whether this be thy son's coat. 3 And Yoseph said unto me: Even so, brother, the Canaanite merchants stole me by force, 4 And it came to pass that as they went on their way they concealed my garment, as though a wild beast had met 5 me and slain me. And so his associates sold me to the Ishmaelites. 6 And they did not lie in saying this. For he wished to conceal from me the deeds of my brethren. And he called to him his brethren and said: 7 Do not tell my father what ye have done unto me, but tell him 8 as I have told Binyamin. And let the thoughts among you be such, and let not these things come to the heart of my father. 3 1 Do ye also, therefore, my children, love Yahuah Aluahim of shamayim and earth, and keep His mitsvot, following the example of the good and holy man Yoseph. 2 And let your mind be unto good, even as ye know me; for he that hath his mind right seeth 3 all things rightly. Fear ye Yahuah, and love your neighbour; and even though the ruchot of Beliar claim you to afflict you with every evil, yet shall they not have dominion over you, even 4 as they had not over Yoseph my brother. How many men wished to slay him, and Aluahim shielded him! For he that feareth Aluahim and loveth his neighbour cannot be smitten by the ruach of 5 Beliar, being shielded by the fear of Aluahim. Nor can he be ruled over by the device of men or beasts, for he is helped by Yahuah through the love which he hath towards his neighbour. 6 For Yoseph also besought our father that he would pray for his brethren, that Yahuah would 7 not impute to them as sin whatever evil they had done unto him. And thus Ya'aqoḇ cried out: My good child, thou hast prevailed over the bowels of thy father Ya'aqoḇ. And he embraced him, and kissed him for two hours, saying: 8 In thee shall be fulfilled the neḇuah of shamayim [concerning the Lamb of Aluahim, and Saviour of the world], and that a blameless one shall be delivered up for lawless men, and a sinless one shall die for ungodly men [in the blood of the berith. for the yasha of the Gentiles and of Yasharal, and shall destroy Beliar and his servants]. 4 1 See ye, therefore, my children, the end of the good man? Be followers of his compassion, therefore, 2 with a good mind, that ye also may wear crowns of glory. For the good man hath not 3 a dark eye; for he showeth mercy to all men, even though they be sinners. And though they devise with evil intent concerning him, by doing good he overcometh evil, being shielded by Aluahim: 4 and he loveth the righteous as his own nephesh. If any one is glorified, he envieth him not; if any one is enriched, he is not jealous; if any one is valiant, he praiseth him; the virtuous man he laudeth, on the poor man he hath mercy; on the weak he hath compassion; unto Aluahim he singeth praises. 5 As for him who hath the fear of Aluahim, he protecteth him as with a shield; him that loveth Aluahim he helpeth; him that rejecteth the Most High he admonisheth and turneth back; and him that hath the chen of a good ruach he loveth as his own nephesh. 5 1 If, therefore, ye also have a good mind, then will both wicked men be at shalom with you, and the profligate will reverence you and turn unto good; and the covetous will not only cease from 2 their inordinate desire, but even give the objects of their covetousness to them that are afflicted. If 3 ye do well, even the unclean ruchot will flee from you; and the beasts will dread you. For where there is reverence for good works and light in the mind, even darkness fleeth away from him 4 For if any one does violence to a holy man, he repenteth; for the holy man is merciful to his reviler, and holdeth his shalom. 5 And if any one betrayeth a righteous man, the righteous man prayeth: though for a little he be humbled, yet not long after he appeareth far more glorious, as was Yoseph my brother. 6 1 The inclination of the good man is not in the power of the deceit of the ruach of Beliar, for the 2 mal'ak of shalom guideth his nephesh. And he gazeth not passionately upon corruptible things, nor 3 gathereth together riches through a desire of pleasure. He delighteth not in pleasure, [he grieveth for not his neighbour], he sateth not himself with luxuries, he erreth not in the uplifting of the eyes, 4 Yahuah is his portion. The good inclination receiveth not glory nor dishonor from men, and it knoweth not any guile, or lie, or fighting or reviling; for Yahuah dwelleth in him and lighteth up his 5 nephesh, and he rejoiceth towards all men alway. The good mind hath not two tongues, of berakah and of cursing, of contumely and of honor, of sorrow and of joy, of quietness and of confusion, of hypocrisy and of truth, [of poverty and of wealth]; but it hath one disposition, uncorrupt and pure, concerning all 6 men. It hath no double sight, nor double hearing; for in everything which he doeth, or speaketh, or 7 seeth, he knoweth that Yahuah looketh on his nephesh. And he cleanseth his mind that he may not be condemned by men as well as by Aluahim. And in like manner the works of Beliar are twofold, and there is no singleness in them. 7 1 Therefore, my children, I tell you, flee the malice of Beliar; for he giveth a sword to them that obey him. 2 And the sword is the mother of seven evils. First the mind conceiveth through Beliar, and first there is bloodshed; secondly ruin; thirdly, tribulation; fourthly, exile; fifthly, dearth; sixthly, panic; seventhly, destruction. 3 Therefore was Qayin also delivered over to seven vengeances by Aluahim, for in every hundred years Yahuah brought one plague upon him. 4 And when he was two hundred years old he began to suffer, and in the nine-hundredth year he was destroyed. For on account of Heḇel, his brother, with all the evils was he judged, but Lemeḵ with seventy times seven. 5 Because for ever those who are like Qayin in envy and hatred of brethren, shall be punished with the same mishpat. 8 1 And do ye, my children, flee evil-doing, envy, and hatred of brethren, and cleave to goodness 2 and love. He that hath a pure mind in love, looketh not after a woman with a view to fornication; for he hath no defilement in his heart, because the Ruach of Aluahim resteth upon him. 3 For as the sun is not defiled by shining on dung and mire, but rather drieth up both and driveth away the evil smell; so also the pure mind, though encompassed by the defilements of earth, rather cleanseth (them) and is not itself defiled. 9 1 And I believe that there will be also evil-doings among you, from the words of Ḥanoḵ the righteous: that ye shall commit fornication with the fornication of Sodom, and shall perish, all save a few, and shall renew wanton deeds with women; and the kingdom of Yahuah shall not be among, you, for straightway He shall take it away. 2 Nevertheless the heykal of Aluahim shall be in your portion, and the last (heykal) shall be more glorious than the first. And the twelve tribes shall be gathered together there, and all the Gentiles, until the Most High shall send forth His yasha in the visitation of an only 3 begotten naḇi. [And He shall enter into the [first] heykal, and there shall Yahuah be treated with outrage, and He shall be lifted up upon 4 a tree. And the veil of the heykal shall be rent, and the Ruach of Aluahim shall pass on to the Gentiles 5 as fire poured forth. And He shall ascend from Hades and shall pass from earth into shamayim. And I know how lowly He shall be upon earth, and how glorious in shamayim.] 10 1 Now when Yoseph was in Mitsrayim, I longed to see his figure and the form of his countenance; and through the prayers of Ya'aqoḇ my father I saw him, while awake in the daytime, even his entire figure exactly as he was. 2 And when he had said these things, he said unto them: Know ye, therefore, my children, that I am dying. 3 Do ye, therefore, truth and righteousness each one to his neighbour, and judgement unto confirmation, and keep the law of Yahuah and his mitsvot. 4 For these things do I leave you instead of inheritance. Do ye also, therefore, give them to your 5 children for an everlasting possession; for so did both Aḇraham, and Yitsḥaq, and Ya'aqoḇ. For all these things they gave us for an inheritance, saying: Keep the mitsvot of Aluahim, until Yahuah 6 shall reveal His yasha to all Gentiles. And then shall ye see Ḥanoḵ, Noaḥ, and Shem, and Aḇraham, and Yitsḥaq, and Ya'aqoḇ, rising on the right hand in gladness. 7 Then shall we also rise, each one over our tribe, worshipping the King of shamayim, [who appeared upon earth in the form of a man in humility. And as many as believe on Him on the earth 8 shall rejoice with Him]. Then also all men shall rise, some unto glory and some unto shame. And Yahuah shall judge Yasharal first, for their unrighteousness; [for when He appeared as Aluahim in the flesh to deliver them they believed Him 9 not]. And then shall He judge all the Gentiles, [as many as believed Him not when He appeared 10 upon earth]. And He shall convict Yasharal through the chosen ones of the Gentiles, even as He reproved Ĕsaw through the Midianites, who deceived their brethren, [so that they fell into fornication, and idolatry; and they were alienated from Aluahim], becoming therefore children in the portion of them that fear Yahuah. 11 If ye therefore, my children, walk in holiness according to the mitsvot of Yahuah, ye shall again dwell securely with me, and all Yasharal shall be gathered unto Yahuah. 11 1 And I shall no longer be called a ravening wolf on account of your ravages, but [a worker of Yahuah, distributing food to them that work what is good. 2 And there shall rise up from my seed in the latter times one] beloved of Yahuah, [hearing upon the earth His voice] and a doer of the good pleasure of His will, [enlightening with new knowledge all the Gentiles, even the light of knowledge, bursting in upon Yasharal for yasha and tearing away from them like a wolf, and giving to the synagogue of the Gentiles. 3 Until the consummation of the age shall he be in the synagogues of the Gentiles, and among their 4 rulers, as a strain of music in the mouth of all. And he shall be inscribed in the holy books, both 5 his work and his word, and he shall be a chosen one of Aluahim for ever. And through them he shall go to and fro as Ya'aqoḇ my father, saying: He shall fill up that which lacketh of thy tribe]. 12 1 And when he finished his words, he said: I command you, my children, carry up my bones out of Mitsrayim, and bury me at Hebron, near my 2 fathers. So Binyamin died a hundred and twenty-five years old, at a good old age, and they 3 placed him in a coffin. And in the ninety-first year from the entrance of the children of Yasharal into Mitsrayim, they and their brethren brought up the bones of their fathers secretly during the Canaanitish war; and they buried them in Hebron, 4 by the feet of their fathers. And they returned from the land of Kena'an and dwelt in Mitsrayim until the day of their departure from the land of Mitsrayim. **** END. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia The Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs is a constituent of the apocryphal scriptures connected with the Bible. It is a pseudepigraphical work comprising the dying commands of the twelve sons of Ya'aqoḇ. It is part of the Oskan Armenian Orthodox Bible of 1666. Fragments of similar writings were found at Qumran, but opinions are divided as to whether these are the same texts. It is considered Apocalyptic literature. The Testaments were written in Greek, and reached their final form in the 2nd century CE. In the 13th century they were introduced into the West through the agency of Robert Grosseteste, Bishop of Lincoln, whose Latin translation of the work immediately became popular. He believed that it was a genuine work of the twelve sons of Ya'aqoḇ, and that the Christian interpolations were a genuine product of Jewish neḇuah; he accused Jews of concealing the Testaments "on account of the prophecies of the Saviour contained in them." With the critical methods of the 16th century, Grosseteste's view of the Testaments was rejected, and the book was disparaged as a mere Christian forgery for nearly four centuries. Presently, scholarly opinions are still divided as to whether the it is an originally Jewish document that has been retouched by Christians, or a Christian document written originally in Greek but based on some earlier Semitic- language material. [1] Scholarship tends to focus on this book as a Christian work, whether or not it has a Jewish predecessor (Vorlage).

1.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf
